column1: title#shloka_in_sanskrit#transliteration_en#translation_en#word_by_word_en

BG 6.1#श्रीभगवानुवाच
अनाश्रित: कर्मफलं कार्यं कर्म करोति य:
स संन्यासी च योगी च न निरग्निर्न चाक्रिय:#śhrī bhagavān uvācha
anāśhritaḥ karma-phalaṁ kāryaṁ karma karoti yaḥ
sa sannyāsī cha yogī cha na niragnir na chākriyaḥ#The Supreme Lord said: Those who perform prescribed duties without desiring the results of their actions are actual sanyāsīs (renunciates) and yogis, not those who have merely ceased performing sacrifices such as Agnihotra yajna or abandoned bodily activities.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Lord said

BG 6.2#यं संन्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव\nन ह्यसंन्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन#yaṁ sannyāsam iti prāhur yogaṁ taṁ viddhi pāṇḍava\nna hyasannyasta-saṅkalpo yogī bhavati kaśhchana#What is known as sanyās is non-different from Yog, for none become yogis without renouncing worldly desires.#yam—what

BG 6.3#आरुरुक्षोर्मुनेर्योगं कर्म कारणमुच्यते\nयोगारूढस्य तस्यैव शम: कारणमुच्यते#ārurukṣhor muner yogaṁ karma kāraṇam uchyate\nyogārūḍhasya tasyaiva śhamaḥ kāraṇam uchyate#To the soul who is aspiring for perfection in Yog, work without attachment is said to be the means
column2: to the sage who is already elevated in Yog, tranquility in meditation is said to be the means.#ārurukṣhoḥ—a beginner
column8: yoga ārūḍhasya—of those who are elevated in Yog

column1: BG 6.4#यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्जते\nसर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते#yadā hi nendriyārtheṣhu na karmasv-anuṣhajjate\nsarva-saṅkalpa-sannyāsī yogārūḍhas tadochyate#When one is neither attached to sense objects nor to actions, such a person is said to be elevated in the science of Yog, having renounced all desires for the fruits of actions.#yadā—when
column8: sarva-saṅkalpa—all desires for the fruits of actions
column10: yoga-ārūḍhaḥ—elevated in the science of Yog

column1: BG 6.5#उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्\nआत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मन:#uddhared ātmanātmānaṁ nātmānam avasādayet\nātmaiva hyātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ#Elevate yourself through the power of your mind, and not degrade yourself, for the mind can be the friend and also the enemy of the self.#uddharet—elevate

BG 6.6#बन्धुरात्मात्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जित:\nअनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्ते तात्मैव शत्रुवत्#bandhur ātmātmanas tasya yenātmaivātmanā jitaḥ\nanātmanas tu śhatrutve vartetātmaiva śhatru-vat#For those who have conquered the mind, it is their friend. For those who have failed to do so, the mind works like an enemy.#bandhuḥ—friend

BG 6.7#जितात्मन: प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहित:\nशीतोष्णसुखदु:खेषु तथा मानापमानयो:#jitātmanaḥ praśhāntasya paramātmā samāhitaḥ\nśhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣhu tathā mānāpamānayoḥ#The yogis who have conquered the mind rise above the dualities of cold and heat, joy and sorrow, and honor and dishonor. Such yogis remain peaceful and steadfast in their devotion to God.#jita-ātmanaḥ—one who has conquered one’s mind

BG 6.8#ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रिय:\nयुक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चन:#jñāna-vijñāna-tṛiptātmā kūṭa-stho vijitendriyaḥ\nyukta ityuchyate yogī sama-loṣhṭāśhma-kāñchanaḥ#The yogi who are satisfied by knowledge and discrimination, and have conquered their senses, remain undisturbed in all circumstances. They see everything—dirt, stones, and gold—as the same.#jñāna—knowledge
column5: vijita-indriyaḥ—one who has conquered the senses
column6: yuktaḥ—one who is in constant communion with the Supreme

column1: BG 6.9#सुहृन्मित्रार्युदासीनमध्यस्थद्वेष्यबन्धुषु\nसाधुष्वपि च पापेषु समबुद्धिर्विशिष्यते#suhṛin-mitrāryudāsīna-madhyastha-dveṣhya-bandhuṣhu\nsādhuṣhvapi cha pāpeṣhu sama-buddhir viśhiṣhyate#The yogis look upon all—well-wishers, friends, foes, the pious, and the sinners—with an impartial intellect. The yogi who is of equal intellect toward friend, companion, and foe, neutral among enemies and relatives, and unbiased between the righteous and sinful, is considered to be distinguished among humans.#su-hṛit—toward the well-wishers

BG 6.10#योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थित:\nएकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रह:#yogī yuñjīta satatam ātmānaṁ rahasi sthitaḥ\nekākī yata-chittātmā nirāśhīr aparigrahaḥ#Those who seek the state of Yog should reside in seclusion, constantly engaged in meditation with a controlled mind and body, getting rid of desires and possessions for enjoyment.#yogī—a yogi
column8: yata-chitta-ātmā—with a controlled mind and body

column1: BG 6.11#शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मन:\nनात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम्#śhuchau deśhe pratiṣhṭhāpya sthiram āsanam ātmanaḥ\nnātyuchchhritaṁ nāti-nīchaṁ chailājina-kuśhottaram#To practice Yog, one should make an āsan (seat) in a sanctified place, by placing Kuśh grass, deer skin, and a cloth, one over the other. The āsan should be neither too high nor too low.#śhuchau—in a clean

BG 6.12#तत्रैकाग्रं मन: कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रिय:
उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये
समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिर:
सम्प्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन्#tatraikāgraṁ manaḥ kṛitvā yata-chittendriya-kriyaḥ
upaviśhyāsane yuñjyād yogam ātma-viśhuddhaye
samaṁ kāya-śhiro-grīvaṁ dhārayann achalaṁ sthiraḥ
samprekṣhya nāsikāgraṁ svaṁ diśhaśh chānavalokayan#BG 6.12-13:#tatra—there
column10: yuñjyāt yogam—should strive to practice yog
column11: ātma viśhuddhaye—for purification of the mind
column20: nāsika-agram—at the tip of the nose

column1:
BG 6.13#तत्रैकाग्रं मन: कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रिय:
उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये
समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिर:
सम्प्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन्#tatraikāgraṁ manaḥ kṛitvā yata-chittendriya-kriyaḥ
upaviśhyāsane yuñjyād yogam ātma-viśhuddhaye
samaṁ kāya-śhiro-grīvaṁ dhārayann achalaṁ sthiraḥ
samprekṣhya nāsikāgraṁ svaṁ diśhaśh chānavalokayan#BG 6.12-13:#tatra—there
column10: yuñjyāt yogam—should strive to practice yog
column11: ātma viśhuddhaye—for purification of the mind
column20: nāsika-agram—at the tip of the nose

column1: BG 6.14#प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थित:\nमन: संयम्य मच्चित्तो युक्त आसीत मत्पर:#praśhāntātmā vigata-bhīr brahmachāri-vrate sthitaḥ\nmanaḥ sanyamya mach-chitto yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ#Thus, with a serene, fearless, and unwavering mind, and staunch in the vow of celibacy, the vigilant yogi should meditate on Me, having Me alone as the supreme goal.#praśhānta—serene
column11: mat-paraḥ—having me as the supreme goal

column1: BG 6.15#युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी नियतमानस:\nशान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति#yuñjann evaṁ sadātmānaṁ yogī niyata-mānasaḥ\nśhāntiṁ nirvāṇa-paramāṁ mat-sansthām adhigachchhati#Thus, constantly keeping the mind absorbed in Me, the yogi of disciplined mind attains nirvāṇ, and abides in Me in supreme peace.#yuñjan—keeping the mind absorbed in God

BG 6.16#नात्यश्नतस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्नत:\nन चाति स्वप्नशीलस्य जाग्रतो नैव चार्जुन#nātyaśhnatastu yogo ’sti na chaikāntam anaśhnataḥ\nna chāti-svapna-śhīlasya jāgrato naiva chārjuna#O Arjun, those who eat too much or too little, sleep too much or too little, cannot attain success in Yog.#na—not
column15: jāgrataḥ—one who does not sleep enough

column1: BG 6.17#युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्मसु\nयुक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दु:खहा#yuktāhāra-vihārasya yukta-cheṣhṭasya karmasu\nyukta-svapnāvabodhasya yogo bhavati duḥkha-hā#But those who are temperate in eating and recreation, balanced in work, and regulated in sleep, can mitigate all sorrows by practicing Yog.#yukta—moderate

BG 6.18#यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते\nनि:स्पृह: सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा#yadā viniyataṁ chittam ātmanyevāvatiṣhṭhate\nniḥspṛihaḥ sarva-kāmebhyo yukta ityuchyate tadā#With thorough discipline, they learn to withdraw the mind from selfish cravings and rivet it on the unsurpassable good of the self. Such persons are said to be in Yog, and are free from all yearning of the senses.#yadā—when

BG 6.19#यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता\nयोगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मन:#yathā dīpo nivāta-stho neṅgate sopamā smṛitā\nyogino yata-chittasya yuñjato yogam ātmanaḥ#Just as a lamp in a windless place does not flicker, so the disciplined mind of a yogi remains steady in meditation on the Supreme.#yathā—as

BG 6.20#यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया\nयत्र चैवात्मनात्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति#yatroparamate chittaṁ niruddhaṁ yoga-sevayā\nyatra chaivātmanātmānaṁ paśhyann ātmani tuṣhyati#When the mind, restrained from material activities, becomes still by the practice of Yog, then the yogi is able to behold the soul through the purified mind, and he rejoices in the inner joy.#yatra—when

BG 6.21#सुखमात्यन्तिकं यत्तद्बुद्धिग्राह्यमतीन्द्रियम्\nवेत्ति यत्र न चैवायं स्थितश्चलति तत्त्वत:#sukham ātyantikaṁ yat tad buddhi-grāhyam atīndriyam\nvetti yatra na chaivāyaṁ sthitaśh chalati tattvataḥ#In that joyous state of Yog, called samadhi, one experiences supreme boundless divine bliss, and thus situated, one never deviates from the Eternal Truth.#sukham—happiness

BG 6.22#यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं तत:\nयस्मिन्स्थितो न दु:खेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते#yaṁ labdhvā chāparaṁ lābhaṁ manyate nādhikaṁ tataḥ\nyasmin sthito na duḥkhena guruṇāpi vichālyate#Having gained that state, one does not consider any attainment to be greater. Being thus established, one is not shaken even in the midst of the greatest calamity.#yam—which

BG 6.23#तं विद्याद् दु:खसंयोगवियोगं योगसञ्ज्ञितम्\nस निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा#taṁ vidyād duḥkha-sanyoga-viyogaṁ yogasaṅjñitam\nsa niśhchayena yoktavyo yogo ’nirviṇṇa-chetasā#That state of severance from union with misery is known as Yog. This Yog should be resolutely practiced with determination free from pessimism.#tam—that
column3: duḥkha-sanyoga-viyogam—state of severance from union with misery

column1:
BG 6.24#सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषत:
मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्तत:
शनै: शनैरुपरमेद्बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया
आत्मसंस्थं मन: कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत्#saṅkalpa-prabhavān kāmāns tyaktvā sarvān aśheṣhataḥ
manasaivendriya-grāmaṁ viniyamya samantataḥ
śhanaiḥ śhanair uparamed buddhyā dhṛiti-gṛihītayā
ātma-sansthaṁ manaḥ kṛitvā na kiñchid api chintayet#BG 6.24-25:#saṅkalpa—a resolve
column16: dhṛiti-gṛihītayā—achieved through determination of resolve that is in accordance with scriptures

column1:
BG 6.25#सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषत:
मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्तत:
शनै: शनैरुपरमेद्बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया
आत्मसंस्थं मन: कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत्#saṅkalpa-prabhavān kāmāns tyaktvā sarvān aśheṣhataḥ
manasaivendriya-grāmaṁ viniyamya samantataḥ
śhanaiḥ śhanair uparamed buddhyā dhṛiti-gṛihītayā
ātma-sansthaṁ manaḥ kṛitvā na kiñchid api chintayet#BG 6.24-25:#saṅkalpa—a resolve
column16: dhṛiti-gṛihītayā—achieved through determination of resolve that is in accordance with scriptures

column1: BG 6.26#यतो यतो निश्चरति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम्\nततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत्#yato yato niśhcharati manaśh chañchalam asthiram\ntatas tato niyamyaitad ātmanyeva vaśhaṁ nayet#Whenever and wherever the restless and unsteady mind wanders, one should bring it back and continually focus it on God.#yataḥ yataḥ—whenever and wherever

BG 6.27#प्रशान्तमनसं ह्येनं योगिनं सुखमुत्तमम्\nउपैति शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतमकल्मषम्#praśhānta-manasaṁ hyenaṁ yoginaṁ sukham uttamam\nupaiti śhānta-rajasaṁ brahma-bhūtam akalmaṣham#Great transcendental happiness comes to the yogi whose mind is calm, whose passions are subdued, who is without sin, and who sees everything in connection with God.#praśhānta—peaceful

BG 6.28#युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी विगतकल्मष:\nसुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्नुते#yuñjann evaṁ sadātmānaṁ yogī vigata-kalmaṣhaḥ\nsukhena brahma-sansparśham atyantaṁ sukham aśhnute#The self-controlled yogi, thus uniting the self with God, becomes free from material contamination, and being in constant touch with the Supreme, achieves the highest state of perfect happiness.#yuñjan—uniting (the self with God)
column9: brahma-sansparśham—constantly in touch with the Supreme

column1: BG 6.29#सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि\nईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शन:#sarva-bhūta-stham ātmānaṁ sarva-bhūtāni chātmani\nīkṣhate yoga-yuktātmā sarvatra sama-darśhanaḥ#The true yogis, uniting their consciousness with God, see with equal eye, all living beings in God and God in all living beings.#sarva-bhūta-stham—situated in all living beings
column8: yoga-yukta-ātmā—one united in consciousness with God

column1: BG 6.30#यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति\nतस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति#yo māṁ paśhyati sarvatra sarvaṁ cha mayi paśhyati\ntasyāhaṁ na praṇaśhyāmi sa cha me na praṇaśhyati#For those who see Me everywhere and see all things in Me, I am never lost, nor are they ever lost to Me.#yaḥ—who

BG 6.31#सर्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थित:\nसर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्तते#sarva-bhūta-sthitaṁ yo māṁ bhajatyekatvam āsthitaḥ\nsarvathā vartamāno ’pi sa yogī mayi vartate#The yogi who is established in union with Me, and worships Me as the Supreme Soul residing in all beings, dwells only in Me, though engaged in all kinds of activities.#sarva-bhūta-sthitam—situated in all beings

BG 6.32#आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन\nसुखं वा यदि वा दु:खं स योगी परमो मत:#ātmaupamyena sarvatra samaṁ paśhyati yo ’rjuna\nsukhaṁ vā yadi vā duḥkhaṁ sa yogī paramo mataḥ#I regard them to be perfect yogis who see the true equality of all living beings and respond to the joys and sorrows of others as if they were their own.#ātma-aupamyena—similar to oneself

BG 6.33#अर्जुन उवाच
योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्त: साम्येन मधुसूदन
एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात्स्थितिं स्थिराम्#arjuna uvācha
yo ’yaṁ yogas tvayā proktaḥ sāmyena madhusūdana
etasyāhaṁ na paśhyāmi chañchalatvāt sthitiṁ sthirām#Arjun said: The system of Yog that you have described, O Madhusudan, appears impractical and unattainable to me, due to the restless mind.#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said
column8: madhu-sūdana—Shree Krishna, the killer of the demon named Madhu

column1: BG 6.34#चञ्चलं हि मन: कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद्दृढम्\nतस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम्#chañchalaṁ hi manaḥ kṛiṣhṇa pramāthi balavad dṛiḍham\ntasyāhaṁ nigrahaṁ manye vāyor iva su-duṣhkaram#The mind is very restless, turbulent, strong and obstinate, O Krishna. It appears to me that it is more difficult to control than the wind.#chañchalam—restless

BG 6.35#श्रीभगवानुवाच
असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलम्
अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते#śhrī bhagavān uvācha
asanśhayaṁ mahā-bāho mano durnigrahaṁ chalam
abhyāsena tu kaunteya vairāgyeṇa cha gṛihyate#Lord Krishna said: O mighty-armed son of Kunti, what you say is correct
column2: the mind is indeed very difficult to restrain. But by practice and detachment, it can be controlled.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—Lord Krishna said

column1: BG 6.36#असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मति:\nवश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्तुमुपायत:#asaṅyatātmanā yogo duṣhprāpa iti me matiḥ\nvaśhyātmanā tu yatatā śhakyo ’vāptum upāyataḥ#Yog is difficult to attain for one whose mind is unbridled. However, those who have learnt to control the mind, and who strive earnestly by proper means, can attain perfection in Yog. This is My opinion.#asanyata-ātmanā—one whose mind is unbridled
column7: vaśhya-ātmanā—by one whose mind is controlled

column1:
BG 6.37#अर्जुन उवाच
अयति: श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्चलितमानस:
अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति#arjuna uvācha
ayatiḥ śhraddhayopeto yogāch chalita-mānasaḥ
aprāpya yoga-sansiddhiṁ kāṅ gatiṁ kṛiṣhṇa gachchhati#Arjun said: What is the fate of the unsuccessful yogi who begins the path with faith, but who does not endeavor sufficiently due to an unsteady mind and is unable to reach the goal of Yog in this life?#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said

BG 6.38#कच्चिन्नोभयविभ्रष्टश्छिन्नाभ्रमिव नश्यति\nअप्रतिष्ठो महाबाहो विमूढो ब्रह्मण: पथि#kachchin nobhaya-vibhraṣhṭaśh chhinnābhram iva naśhyati\napratiṣhṭho mahā-bāho vimūḍho brahmaṇaḥ pathi#Does not such a person who deviates from Yog get deprived of both material and spiritual success, O mighty-armed Krishna, and perish like a broken cloud with no position in either sphere?#kachchit—whether

BG 6.39#एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषत:\nत्वदन्य: संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न ह्युपपद्यते#etan me sanśhayaṁ kṛiṣhṇa chhettum arhasyaśheṣhataḥ\ntvad-anyaḥ sanśhayasyāsya chhettā na hyupapadyate#O Krishna, please dispel this doubt of mine completely, for who other than You can do so?#etat—this

BG 6.40#श्रीभगवानुवाच
पार्थ नैवेह नामुत्र विनाशस्तस्य विद्यते
न हि कल्याणकृत्कश्चिद्दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति#śhrī bhagavān uvācha
pārtha naiveha nāmutra vināśhas tasya vidyate
na hi kalyāṇa-kṛit kaśhchid durgatiṁ tāta gachchhati#The Supreme Lord said: O Parth, one who engages on the spiritual path does not meet with destruction either in this world or the world to come. My dear friend, one who strives for God-realization is never overcome by evil.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Lord said

BG 6.41#प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वती: समा:
शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते
अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम्
एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम्#prāpya puṇya-kṛitāṁ lokān uṣhitvā śhāśhvatīḥ samāḥ
śhuchīnāṁ śhrīmatāṁ gehe yoga-bhraṣhṭo ’bhijāyate
atha vā yoginām eva kule bhavati dhīmatām
etad dhi durlabhataraṁ loke janma yad īdṛiśham#BG 6.41-42:#prāpya—attain
column13: yoginām—of those endowed with divine wisdom

column1:
BG 6.42#प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वती: समा:
शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते
अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम्
एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम्#prāpya puṇya-kṛitāṁ lokān uṣhitvā śhāśhvatīḥ samāḥ
śhuchīnāṁ śhrīmatāṁ gehe yoga-bhraṣhṭo ’bhijāyate
atha vā yoginām eva kule bhavati dhīmatām
etad dhi durlabhataraṁ loke janma yad īdṛiśham#BG 6.41-42:#prāpya—attain
column13: yoginām—of those endowed with divine wisdom

column1: BG 6.43#तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्वदेहिकम्\nयतते च ततो भूय: संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन#yatate cha tato bhūyaḥ sansiddhau kuru-nandana#On taking such a birth, O descendant of Kurus, they reawaken the wisdom of their previous lives, and strive even harder toward perfection in Yog.#tatra—there

BG 6.44#पूर्वाभ्यासेन तेनैव ह्रियते ह्यवशोऽपि स:\nजिज्ञासुरपि योगस्य शब्दब्रह्मातिवर्तते#pūrvābhyāsena tenaiva hriyate hyavaśho ’pi saḥ\njijñāsur api yogasya śhabda-brahmātivartate#Indeed, they feel drawn toward God, even against their will, on the strength of their past discipline. Such seekers naturally rise above the ritualistic principles of the scriptures.#pūrva—past

BG 6.45#प्रयत्नाद्यतमानस्तु योगी संशुद्धकिल्बिष:\nअनेकजन्मसंसिद्धस्ततो याति परां गतिम्#prayatnād yatamānas tu yogī sanśhuddha-kilbiṣhaḥ\naneka-janma-sansiddhas tato yāti parāṁ gatim#With the accumulated merits of many past births, when these yogis engage in sincere endeavors to make further progress, they become purified from material desires and attain perfection in this life itself.#prayatnāt—with great effort

BG 6.46#तपस्विभ्योऽधिकोयोगी
ज्ञानिभ्योऽपिमतोऽधिक:
कर्मिभ्यश्चाधिकोयोगी
तस्माद्योगीभवार्जुन#tapasvibhyo ’dhiko yogī
jñānibhyo ’pi mato ’dhikaḥ
karmibhyaśh chādhiko yogī
tasmād yogī bhavārjuna#A yogi is superior to the tapasvī (ascetic), superior to the jñānī (a person of learning), and even superior to the karmī (ritualistic performer). Therefore, O Arjun, strive to be a yogi.#tapasvibhyaḥ—than the ascetics

BG 6.47#योगिनामपि सर्वेषां मद्गतेनान्तरात्मना\nश्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मत:#yoginām api sarveṣhāṁ mad-gatenāntar-ātmanā\nśhraddhāvān bhajate yo māṁ sa me yuktatamo mataḥ#Of all yogis, those whose minds are always absorbed in Me, and who engage in devotion to Me with great faith, them I consider to be the highest of all.#yoginām—of all yogis
